item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sometimes this unnatural order of things throws you a softball, and this ball might as well be made out of mozzarella.

…And that's when I knew I'd +1 this. Awesome SCP, I thought it was hilarious! The 'a goblin stole my nether regions' excuse is much funnier than it should be. I think you did a great job!

item.title: Ashur has gotten his own SCP Oversimplified page!
item.content:encoded: Congrats DJKaktus!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god kaktus, this killed me so badly. It's the perfect perfect perfect combination of clinical, foundation procedures and silly adaptive antics, and the dialogue is just perfect. And can we talk about the ending? This rivals 4444 as one of my favorite character SCPs of all time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 100% murdered me. I heard every bit of Ashur's dialogue in a head voice belonging to nobody I've ever known but that is unmistakably his voice. An absolute riot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The godess of minor annoyances! Your little sister!
Her name is a minor memetic that will always be perceived by the subject to be their little sister's name. If they do not possess one, the most irritating personage in their life's name will be substituted. This has lead to a significant number of people hearing the godess's name to be Dr.Bright, and Dr.King to hear it as Apples. The latter had to be tazed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So i know nergal is a god of war and destruction buuuuuuut, Common Cold! Influenza! Nasty Rash! Distemper!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd always headcanoned that D-class assigned to be his servants are volunteers.

My reasoning
Hide my reasoning

If I were in that position, I'd be like: "I'm being offered a chance to be assigned to a reasonably friendly humanoid SCP, do basic chores and eat good food and possibly get laid. In the event I end up sleeping with the guy, it'll probably be disappointing cause he's a thoughtless tool. But it sounds like an overall pleasant time, and if I don't take this opportunity, the next SCP up on the roster for me might kill me or worse."

There'd be no shortage of volunteers. And the Foundation wouldn't send people in to get assaulted unless it were absolutely necessary for containment, especially post-Ethics committee.

Plus, bit of an irl nitpick, but very very few women are ever convicted of rape. The US legal system is not great at identifying or acknowledging the existence of female rapists, and I doubt other countries are much better. And it's strongly implied here that Ashur is heterosexual.

But I agree the body pillows would definitely fool him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Imagine just being a D-Class that was a serial rapist on death row and being assigned to “concubine duty for a really gullible and powerful entity” lmao
Or maybe they just buy him a collection of provocative body pillows and cut a hole in it for his “God Rod”

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely loved the article, especially the epithets of the researchers and how well fleshed out this gullible god is in the memos, direct speech in the transcripts and how Seun talks about him in the transcripts.

Here's your score-of-400 upvote +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
that Nazarene wino with the fish obsession has been flaking for like two thousand years,

Now I know not to trust Jesus with my children, thank you Seun, very cool!

item.title: Novote. I feel like a god-tier party pooper here.
item.content:encoded:
Nitpicks
– hide block


Little things that didn't affect my vote:

  • SCP-3740 is referred to as an "it" in the containment procedures and description, and as "he/him" mostly after that in the non-transcript text.
  • "I mean, it's just… it's just all like… it's all smooth down there." "He means his dick." Why in the world would they leave this stuff in a legitimate file?
  • "Preface: The following document is an excerpt from an internal memo between members of the Site-81 containment research team." The memo is addressed to individuals not internal to Site-81.
  • The allusion to Dr. Oppenheimer and the Manhattan project was nice, but I'm not sure about it coming from SCP-3740. It's possible SCP-3740 could be privy to (relatively) current events.
Positives
– hide block
  • The humorous, clever, and superb quality writing is as charming as Ashur.
  • A really creative twist on containment procedures.
  • A very memorable, likable, and successful character.
  • Some light-hearted scenes and bantering in a Foundation that needs that, bonus being that it's part of their job.
  • The epithets are gold.
  • The inversion of humans kinda (mis)leading an ancient humanoid god for a change.
Negatives
– hide block
  • Profanity used as brazenly as it is in the memo by a significant person (Dr. McElroy) addressed to lots of other significant people would only be believable to me in a -J. The pretermission of it here bothers me.
  • While SCP-3470's gullibility is what makes this so fun and charming, the containment strategy built around it is left precarious to a flaw. For example:
    • If SCP-3740 became hell-bent on going outside and get some fresh air (for example), who could stop it, and (as mentioned) it could still take the rest of the contained anomalies in Site-81 with it.
    • Why isn't it curious about the outside world, given its desire for adventure and stories, happy as it is with the accommodations? This is briefly addressed ("Why would I ever want to leave such a palace?"), but it isn't a very strong treatment; there are countless reasons why it would still want to leave, if only temporarily.

The situation isn't something I think the Foundation as we know would be comfortable with, at least not without an additional failsafe or apparatus in place (the great line about the mozzarella notwithstanding). I wondered if it was just impossible to contain a Class VIII, but there's the line about "the big guns", indicating that they could do something more and maybe have in the past. So I think it's unavoidable that they would want to bust out the big guns here anyway.

Instead of this being humorously inconsequential as it would be in a -J, it's instead a handwave of some fundamental concerns. An attempt is made to explain the concerns away because this issue is the fulcrum of the article's premise and believability, but it's taken at face value by the Foundation as unquestioningly as Ashur would.

Verdict
– hide block

I'm not sure why this isn't a -J; all its issues would have been solved in being one. It is really funny, but makes compromises with logic and believability to that end. It doesn't so much walk the line between a mainlist article and a -J as it jumps back and forth between the two with glee, and that gives it its strengths but also its problems. Imo it overcooks the -J components and misses that -J/mainlist hybrid recipe (as other works of the author have done very well, e.g. SCP-1781, SCP-527, or SCP-4444), and the article becomes less convincing for both in-universe and the site's standards as it progresses.

I enjoyed this a lot and I feel like I'm the un-fun one here, but I alternated between a + and a novote in the same way this article alternates between the posturing of a mainlist article and a -J. The interference was offsetting for me, and I ended on a novote.

item.title: My New Favourite SCP
item.content:encoded: I haven't had this much enjoyment reading an SCP for a lo00ong time. This made me giggle something chronic, especially that last log. Nice work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of the funniest -J 's that… aren't a -J?

+

item.title: The s#!+ I see on this website
item.content:encoded:
This one is getting bookmarked. I guffaw-ed several times out loud at this SCP. I need to see that podcast.

So, does Ashur have Downs or did he dare Gilgamesh to hit him in the head with SCP-3728 a fourth time?

Take your +1 and get the Irkalla out of my sight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
MTF Omega-3740 "Suen"
Every now and then, but not too often, just have a foundation personnel assigned to SCP-3740 go somewhere dangerous and call Suen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, this is hilarious. I don't usually laugh out loud, but while reading this one I had to try very hard not to.

item.title: Re: Fussing
item.content:encoded:
Whole foundation patheon will claim that individual to be "stealler of powers" who manage to escape prison of whatever the hell foundation get him from.
sedating him out, claiming to return stolen powers to original deity, then introducing another ressearcher able to perform the same feat the D-class did.
Of course our fellow god will never see the D class traitor again…

Sorry for necro posting but this needed answer in case someone would try to make it a lore.

Anyway this SCP is gold. I beg you to produce such quality material in future as well.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have olive skin. Its significantly lighter than actual olives.

It's the color of someone who is a pale tanned person. (Usually either arab or of mixed race)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic. I am relatively new, and the number of reality warpers has made me really hesitant to even want to do my SCP (not a reality warper but KIND of close). I feel like this is one of the BEST takes on this whole website for realistic containment procedures for a delusional god entity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can tell that kak had as much fun writing this as I reading it. Truly one of the most delightful mainlisters I’ve ever seen. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"that Nazarene wino with the fish obsession"

I wonder what 343 would say about that one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the author's best efforts. +1 enthsiastically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would read a full novel of this SCP. Clearly a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I only became a member very recently, I've been reading SCP's for about 5 years, and I've not really come across something so amusing and light-hearted yet.

I enjoyed the use of actual Gods, and if you came up with the names (eg. The Horror of Hadrians Hell) yourself, then that's seriously funny and creative, even if the base concept was inspired by a podcast.

This would make an excellent short film.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Haha this SCP article is amazing!

I love it so much, keep up the good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this quite a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Impossible. Not once in his entire letter did he use the word "boy".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You had me at this point — because it was at this point I realized that the entire skip is just an alternate universe where, upon being asked by Gozer if they were Gods, the Ghostbusters said yes.

I am with TheGreatHippo here. Thought of that pretty soon into reading!
Here's the story. I went to the top rated pages this month. Clicked on this. Scrolled down quickly and went 'Oh no. Another long one!' … but catching things here and there while scrolling, I ended up reading… Laughing… Upvoting.

Then everyone proceeds to call him "Ashur" for the rest of the article.

Kaim, this didn't bother me at all. You can easily imagine after "a few" drinks Ashur The Almighty God of the Windswept Plains and Soaring Skies would tell anyone 'C'mon, we're buddies now, call me Ashur'…

A few tiny nitpicks:
- 'olive skin'? Isn't this a rather darkish skin? Because the very, very cute guy in the picture isn't dark-skinned at all…!
- about the picture… Wonder what the REAL guy in it would think if he found out he's a gullible god? ;>
- considering the length of this skip and the amount of work that went into it, can't we upvote several times? Four? Okay, three? Come ON! Two, then - no more no less. Please? Just this once?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lol'd at "Nazarene wino."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean, this should warrant reclassification to 3740-J, because it's fucking hilarious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But know that you can rest easy, because the most dangerous entity at Site-81 thinks Director Aktus is a supernatural space all-father named “Maltheus, the Horror of Hadrian’s Hell” because he knows how to turn on a lightswitch.

Ladies and Gents, This is where I keeled over laughing (followed by many more chuckles later, but this was my favourite.) +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I gotta admit, I enjoy the bit with Jesus there. Kinda hilarious in a way, and makes me think God, aka SCP-343, decided that if Ashur was such a piss poor excuse for a god, that He should step up to the plate and actually do something for mankind. That might also be why God decided to say he was the only one, if every decided to just take turns instead of making it a group effort.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really hate you now for linking that shit. I blame myself a bit for not reading on, but mostly you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of several other mainlists and -J's, like the one that can only be contained by using fake scientific procedures, and the one who becomes your greatest fear, but we've convinced him it's the Robeast.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a newbie here in SCP, I'm in awe. I can definitely learn from you on how to create a great SCP entry.

Have my +1. Have all of my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Under no circumstances are any individuals to address SCP-3740 as anything other than "Ashur, God of the Windswept Plains and Soaring Skies", "Most Victorious and Unchallenged Lord Deific Ashur", or simply, "Mightiest Ashur".

Then everyone proceeds to call him "Ashur" for the rest of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh. Well that's something, I guess. I don't think I get why being a Chad would be a bad thing but whatever works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I greatly enjoy this one a lot!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
prob talking about the "virgin vs chad" meme

(there may be some nsfw in the google images just warning)

also the definition for chad according to urban dictionary

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Chad-like?

item.title: Re: Fussing
item.content:encoded: Then we have a problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is important, we have to know

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cute! +1.

Image feels odd, though. Too common and chadlike for someone as eccentric as Ashur.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is kinda amazing. It reads like if Loki decided to write an SCP-Expy of Thor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't realize it until I read this, but I did the exact same thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If your intention with this was to make the likes of me hurt their faces by the sheer force of their hands crashing against their cranium, then you got that completely right.

Now, seriously I need to think this over. It was really goofy and entertaining tor ead but it fell a little bit short overall. Especially Ashur's last line. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is Suen's reference to "babysitting" Ashur a pun on Suen's alternative name "Nanna"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never thought I would see a shout-out to the genital-stealing goblins in an actual SCP article!

item.title: Re: Fussing
item.content:encoded: and what happens when a D-class demonstrates that "look, I'm double-jointed!"

item.title: Re: Fussing
item.content:encoded: Because, as they are slaves, he will not believe their lies about his fellow gods.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of those SCPs that works well as an actual SCP, and could have worked just as well as a Joke SCP. As gullible and idiotic as he is though, imagine the havoc that would occur if, oh, lets say a member of the chaos insurgency tells him this is all a lie. That'd be a bad day to be on site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was on the fence about this until I got to 'shotgunning a beer'. There's not even the pretense of supernatural power to that 'trick'. Ashur's just a very stupid oddball who gets really excited about anything mildly impressive. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just one thing, djkaktus

Class VIII humanoid realit- altering entity

Did you mean to say reality-altering?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is not only very good, but also does the material that inspired you justice. Bliss Delight, Jesus Christ….

I don't know what it says about me that this is what finally got me into the forums here after reading for so many years. You've got my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I LOVE JESUS AND HIS TERRIBLE SCYTHE LIKE BIBLE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Look now how the demon begs. Thus is the might of Ashur.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"This Site, I like it. ANOTHER!"

XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreeing that this feels formulaic in its structure, and the initial description reminded me of a pre-mass edit SCP in some spots, but when I got to the line about gullibility and read through the logs I found what I got to be thoroughly enjoyable. I guess the McElroy brothers inspired this based on comments but this also definitely gave me some Hello From The Magic Tavern vibes as well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

This was a lot of fun. I read Ashur's voice in my head as Thor's voice.

It also reminded me a bit of my own SCP-3999-J.

item.title: Fussing
item.content:encoded:
Shotgunning a beer and spinning a ball on your fingertip are too obviously things normal humans can do.

And someone whose claim to godliness is being able to lick his elbow should clearly be named "Ulnar", not "Ulmar".

(https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ulna)

and most importantly

how do we keep the D-class from spilling the beans?
